fuzzy_prd:
Hey team, we need to build out this mobile ads adapter layer that sits between our app code and the native ad SDKs. Basically right now every team that wants to show ads has to write all this boilerplate to talk to the platform channel, handle the async callbacks, track which ad is which, etc. It's a mess and every squad is doing it differently.

The adapter should handle the full lifecycle — setting up targeting preferences (think privacy stuff, content ratings, test devices), kicking off the SDK init and reporting back what adapters came up, loading different ad formats with their own flavors of request data, and then dispatching the right callbacks when platform events come back in.

One thing that came up in the Android vs iOS review — remember how we handled that unit normalization issue in the analytics pipeline last quarter? We need something similar here for the timing values that come back from the two platforms, they're in different units and need to be reconciled before surfacing to callers.

Also the inline ad widgets have that double-mount footgun we've seen cause crashes in production — we need the adapter to guard against that cleanly without blowing up the host runtime.

For the gateway codec piece, make sure all the structured types (requests, sizes, errors, reward payloads) survive a round-trip cleanly. The ad registry needs to hand out stable numeric IDs starting from zero and keep track of what's loaded. Full-screen ads that aren't loaded yet should fail gracefully. Ping me if the scope is unclear.

hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Android gateway returns latency as integer milliseconds; divide by 1000 to get seconds (e.g., 23 → 0.023). iOS gateway returns latency already in seconds as a float; preserve it as-is (e.g., 23 → 23.0). Null latency must be rendered as the literal string 'null' on both platforms.

The ad registry must allocate numeric integer IDs starting from 0 for the first loaded ad in a fresh session. Each subsequent load in the same session increments by 1. The registry must support lookup by id (registry_ad_id) and existence check (registry_has_ad). After disposal, registry_has_ad must be false and registry_ad_id must render as 'null'.

trigger_keywords: platform channel label for config update
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The request configuration output must include 'channel=mobile_ad_gateway' and 'method=MobileAds#updateRequestConfiguration'. Fields must appear in this order: channel, method, max_content_rating, child_directed, under_age, test_device_ids. Multiple test device IDs are comma-separated with no spaces.

Initialization output must include 'channel=mobile_ad_gateway', 'method=MobileAds#initialize', then 'adapter_count=N' where N is the number of adapter statuses returned, followed by per-adapter fields: adapter, state, description, latency_seconds. Null latency renders as the literal string 'null'.

Gateway load method names are: 'loadBannerAd' for banner format, 'loadNativeAd' for native format, 'loadInterstitialAd' for interstitial/full-screen format. The dispose method is 'disposeAd' and the show method is 'showAdWithoutView'. These exact strings must appear in output.

trigger_keywords: native ad request vs publisher request
standard request null in native load
request field rendering in load output
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: When loading a native ad: if a standard AdRequest is provided, output 'request=standard' and 'publisher_request=null'; if a PublisherAdRequest is provided, output 'request=null' and 'publisher_request=publisher'. Both fields must always appear for native ads. For banner ads only 'request=standard' appears (no publisher_request line).

Attempting to mount the same ad object or the same widget in two active locations must produce a neutral error output with 'error=ad_widget_already_mounted' and a user-facing summary string. The host runtime exception class name must NOT appear in the output. The error is non-fatal and must not crash the adapter.

trigger_keywords: fullscreen ad show before load error
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Attempting to show a full-screen ad that has not been loaded must output 'error=ad_not_loaded' followed by the affected ad unit identifier. Internal exception details or class names must not be included in the output.
Rendering an inline ad widget on Android must output 'widget_result=platform_view_link' followed by 'view_type=plugins.flutter.io/google_mobile_ads/ad_widget'. These exact strings are required.

trigger_keywords: map and list serialization in publisher request
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: For publisher_request codec output: 'custom_targeting' map entries are rendered as 'key:value' pairs separated by commas. 'custom_targeting_lists' map entries are rendered as 'key:value1,value2' where list values are comma-joined. Each map entry is separated by commas. Keywords list and test_devices list are comma-separated with no spaces.

trigger_keywords: event callback output for loaded event
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: For 'loaded' event: output 'callback=loaded', 'ad_id=N', 'ad_unit_id=...'. For 'failed_to_load' event: output 'callback=failed_to_load', 'ad_id=N', 'code=...', 'domain=...', 'message=...'. For 'native_clicked' event: output 'callback=native_clicked', 'ad_id=N', 'ad_unit_id=...'. The ad must be loaded into the registry before the event is injected.

trigger_keywords: boolean rendering in gateway value round-trip
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: AdRequest codec output must include in order: payload=ad_request, keywords (comma-separated), content_url, birthday_year, gender, designed_for_families (true/false lowercase), child_directed (true/false lowercase), test_devices (comma-separated), non_personalized_ads (true/false lowercase). Booleans must remain booleans rendered as lowercase 'true' or 'false', not integers.

trigger_keywords: how should I organize the code
should I split into multiple files
should I separate business logic from IO
should business logic touch stdin stdout
how to separate parsing from logic
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Scale-driven code organization: match physical structure to domain complexity. For micro-utilities or simple scripts a single well-organized file is fine. For systems with multiple distinct responsibilities (I/O routing, business rules, formatters, etc.) you MUST use a multi-file layout (e.g. src/, tests/) — never a single god file. Do not over-engineer simple problems, but strictly avoid monolithic files for complex domains. Strict separation of concerns is mandatory: core business logic must be completely decoupled from stdin/stdout and JSON parsing. The JSON test cases are a black-box contract for the execution adapter only — NOT the internal data model. Follow SOLID: SRP (separate parsing, routing, core execution, output formatting); OCP (core engine open for extension, closed for modification); DIP (high-level modules depend on abstractions, not I/O details). The public interface must be idiomatic. Errors must be modeled properly (typed exceptions or Result/Monad patterns), not generic faults.

trigger_keywords: how does the test harness call my code
what is the entry point for testing
how does the runner call the program
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: You must implement an execution adapter (dispatcher) that is logically and ideally physically separate from the core domain. It reads a single JSON object from stdin, inspects the 'action' field to route to the correct core function, then prints the result to stdout matching the per-feature output contract. All policy durations in inputs are in seconds; all time points are epoch milliseconds. The adapter must not contain any business logic — every numeric result and boolean decision is delegated to the core domain.

Deliver a bash test harness at rcb_tests/test.sh. It must accept --cases-dir <subdir> (default: test_cases) to point at a directory of *.json case files under rcb_tests/. For each case file it runs every case in the 'cases' array against the dispatcher, writing raw stdout (no PASS/FAIL metadata) to: rcb_tests/stdout/<cases-dir>/{filename_stem}@{case_index_zero_padded_3}.txt Example: rcb_tests/stdout/public_test_cases/feature1_next_retry_at@000.txt Output is namespaced by <cases-dir> so different directories never overwrite each other. A single entry point 'bash rcb_tests/test.sh' runs the full suite with the default dir.

trigger_keywords: can I see the test inputs
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Yes, public test cases are provided, one file per feature. Ask me for a specific feature by name and I will give you the full test case JSON.

trigger_keywords: what are the valid action field values
what actions does the dispatcher handle
which action strings does the adapter support
what are the possible action inputs
tell me all the action names
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The dispatcher routes on the 'action' field. Valid values are: 'dispose_loaded_banner', 'show_loaded_fullscreen'. Each value maps to one feature.

context_pointers.vague_reference: remember how we handled that unit normalization issue in the analytics pipeline last quarter
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Feature 3 (feature3_adapter_status_codec.json): Android latency integers are milliseconds and must be divided by 1000 to produce seconds; iOS latency numerics are already seconds and must be preserved as float. See the AdapterStatus codec logic.

context_pointers.vague_reference: that double-mount footgun we've seen cause crashes in production
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Feature 8 (feature8_ad_widget_mounting.json): The AdWidget must detect when the same ad object is already mounted and raise an 'ad_widget_already_mounted' domain error instead of propagating a raw host-language exception.

context_pointers.vague_reference: the compatibility logic from that login module we did last time
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The platform-channel serialization pattern used in the gateway codec (Feature 4): structured types like AdRequest, PublisherAdRequest, AdSize, LoadAdError, and RewardItem must be encoded and decoded symmetrically, preserving all public fields including nested lists and maps.

oracle_data.fallback_response: That detail isn't something I have documented in the original ticket. You'll need to check the test case JSON files under rcb_tests/public_test_cases/ for the exact expected output format, or look at the PRD section for the relevant feature to find the field ordering and naming conventions.

feature1_request_configuration.json.description: Request-wide targeting configuration is forwarded to the native advertising gateway with all configured privacy, rating, and test-device fields.

feature2_initialization_status.json.description: Initialization invokes the native gateway once and returns each mediation adapter status with state, description, and latency fields.

cases.expected_output:
channel=mobile_ad_gateway
method=MobileAds#initialize
adapter_count=1
adapter=aName
state=not_ready
description=desc
latency_seconds=null

feature3_adapter_status_codec.json.description: Adapter status messages preserve state and description while normalizing Android millisecond latency to seconds and leaving iOS latency values in seconds.

feature4_gateway_value_codec.json.description: Structured values sent through the platform gateway retain their public fields after encoding and decoding, including request metadata, ad sizes, load errors, and reward credits.

cases.expected_output:
payload=ad_request
keywords=1,2,3
content_url=contentUrl
birthday_year=2020
gender=unknown
designed_for_families=true
child_directed=true
test_devices=Android,iOS
non_personalized_ads=false

payload=publisher_request
keywords=who
content_url=dat
custom_targeting=boy:who
custom_targeting_lists=him:is
non_personalized_ads=true

feature5_load_ad_requests.json.description: Loading each supported ad format allocates a stable ad id, forwards a format-specific gateway method with the supplied request data, and makes the ad retrievable by id.

cases.expected_output:
method=loadBannerAd
ad_id=0
ad_unit_id=ca-app-pub-3940256099942544/2934735716
request=standard
size=320x50
registry_ad_id=0
registry_has_ad=true

method=loadNativeAd
ad_id=0
ad_unit_id=ca-app-pub-3940256099942544/3986624511
request=standard
publisher_request=null
factory_id=0
custom_options=a:1,b:2
registry_ad_id=0
registry_has_ad=true

method=loadNativeAd
ad_id=0
ad_unit_id=test-id
request=null
publisher_request=publisher
factory_id=0
custom_options=a:1,b:2
registry_ad_id=0
registry_has_ad=true

feature6_ad_lifecycle_commands.json.description: A loaded ad can be disposed or shown through the gateway by id; attempting to show a full-screen ad before it has been loaded is reported as a neutral ad-not-loaded error.

feature7_ad_event_callbacks.json.description: Platform events addressed by ad id invoke the matching lifecycle callback and expose event payloads such as load errors, reward credits, and load-state transitions.

feature8_ad_widget_mounting.json.description: An in-line ad widget renders as a platform view for the active mobile platform, and the same ad object cannot be mounted in two widget locations at the same time.
